title: The One With the Dozen Lasagnas

lines.speaker: With Minor Adjustments by
lines.text: Dan Silverstein

Central Perk, everyone is there. Ross working on crossword puzzle, starts humming theme from The Odd Couple. Chandler joins in, followed by Monica and Phoebe, then the whole gang. Ross starts humming theme from I Dream Of Jeannie.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No-no-no-no, we're done.
Opening Credits
Monica and Rachel's, Monica is on the phone in the kitchen.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aunt Syl, stop yelling! All I'm saying is that if you had told me vegetarian lasagna, I would have made vegetarian lasagna. (pauses, listens to person on phone) Well, the meat's only every third layer, maybe you could scrape.
Camera moves to Chandler, Phoebe, Ross, and Joey sitting in living room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ross, did you really read all these baby books?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yup! You could plunk me down in the middle of any woman's uterus, no compass, and I can find my way out of there like that! (snaps fingers)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, this is cool...it says in some parts of the world, people actually eat the placenta. (Joey grimaces)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And, we're done with the yogurt. (Sets yogurt down on table)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (softly) Sorry. (Camera pans back to Monica, still on phone)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Aunt Syl, I did this as a favor, I am not a caterer. What do you want me to do with a dozen lasagnas? (listens to Aunt Syl on phone, looks shocked) Nice talk, Aunt Syl. (in New York accent) You kiss Uncle Freddie with that mouth?
Camera pans back to group in living room

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Ross, listen, you know that right now, your baby's only this big? (measures about 2 inches with his thumb and index finger) This is your baby. (in baby-like voice) Hi Daddy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (waves) Hello!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (in baby-like voice) How come you don't live with Mommy? (pause; shows Ross less than amused) How come Mommy lives with that other lady? (pause; Ross still looks less than amused; Joey smiling) What's a lesbian? (playfully hits Ross)
Rachel enters with Paolo, speaking Italian. Ross looks annoyed

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Honey, you can say it, Poconos, Poconos, it's like Poc-o-nos (touching Paolo's nose with forefinger with each syllable)

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Ah, poke (Paolo touches Rachel's nose) a (touches nose again) nose, mmm (they rub noses, then kisses her)

lines.speaker: Joey, Chandler, and Ross
lines.text: (sitting in living room, imitating Paolo) Mma, Mma, Mmaah
Camera pans to Rachel, Monica, and Phoebe in the kitchen

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So, did I hear Poconos?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes, my sister's giving us her place for the weekend.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Woo-hoo, first weekend away together!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yeah, that's a big step.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I know...
Camera pans to Ross, looking dejected

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Ross) Ah, it's just a weekend, big deal!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wasn't this supposed to be just a fling, huh? Shouldn't it be...(makes flinging motions with hands) flung by now?
Camera pans back to Rachel

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I mean, we are way past the fling thing, I mean, I am feeling things that I've only read about in Danielle Steele books, you know? I mean, when I'm with him, I'm totally, totally...
Camera pans to Ross, holding his stomach

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...nauseous, I'm physically nauseous. What am I supposed to do, huh? Call immigration? (pauses, looks suddenly inspired) I could call immigration!
The Hallway, Chandler and Joey leaving girls' apartment, carrying lasagna.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I love babies, with their little baby shoes, and their little baby toes, and their little baby hands...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, you're going to have to stop that, forever!
Joey opens door, throws keys on kitchen table, table falls over

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Need a new table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You think?
Carol and Susan's, there's a knock on the door and Carol answers it to Ross.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hey hey, come on in!
Ross enters, carrying lasagna

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, hello! mmwa! (kisses Carol) I brought all the books, and Monica sends her love, along with this lasagna.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Oh great! Is it vegetarian, 'cause Susan doesn't eat meat.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pauses) I'm pretty sure that it is...

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: So, I got the results of the amnio today.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (making flinging gestures with hands) Oh, tell me, tell me, is everything, uhh....?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Totally and completely healthy!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, that's great, that is great! (Hugs and kisses Carol. Then picks up a picture frame)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey, when did you and Susan meet Huey Lewis?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Uh, that's our friend Tanya.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (surprised, chuckling nervously) Of course it's your friend Tanya. (looks up frightenedly)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Don't you want to know about the sex?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (chuckles nervously) The sex? (chuckles) Um, I'm having enough trouble with the image of you and Susan together, when you throw in Tanya (miming washing hair, that's the best I could think of), yaw...

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: The sex of the baby, Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, you know the sex of the baby? Oh, oh-oh-oh!

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Do you want to know?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no, no, no, no, I don't want to know, absolutely not. I think, you know, I think you should know until you look down there, and say, oop, there it is! (pauses) Or isn't...
Susan enters

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, hello Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Susan...

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: So, so, did you hear?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yes, we did, everything's A-OK!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, that's so... (Susan hugs Carol, they giggle, Ross steps away) It really is...do we know...?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Yes, we certainly do, it's going to be...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (flailing arms in protest) Oh, hey hey hey, ho ho ho, hello, guy who doesn't want to know, standing right here!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, well, is it what we thought it would be?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Mm-hmmm (Susan and Carol hug, giggling. Ross stands back, reaches out and lightly taps Susan's shoulder)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ok, what, what...ok, what did we think it was going to be?

lines.speaker: Carol and Susan
lines.text: It's a...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupts) No, no, no I don't want to know, don't want to know. Ok, you know, I should probably, I should probably just go.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Well, thanks for the books.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No problem, ok, mmmwa (kisses Carol) oh, mmmwa (kisses Carol's stomach, then punches Susan's shoulder) Susan... (Ross leaves.)

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: All right, who should we call first, your folks, or Deb and Rona? (intercom buzzer rings)

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Hello?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (on intercom) Uh, never mind, I don't want to know. (Carol and Susan laugh)
Chandler and Joey's,  Joey and Chandler use their knees as a table to support the lasagna.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ok, so it's just because it was my table, I have to buy a new one?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: That's the rule.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What rule? There's no rule, if anything, you owe me a table!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: How'd you get to that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I believe the piece of furniture was fine until your little breakfast adventure with Angela Delvecchio

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You knew about that?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, let's just say the impressions you made in the butter left little to the imagination.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Ok, ok, How about if we split it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What do you mean, like, buy it together?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You think we're ready for something like that?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it's a pretty big commitment, I mean, what if one of us wants to move out?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Why, are you moving out?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm not moving out.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You'd tell me if you were moving out right

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, it's just that with my last roommate Kip...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Aw, I know all about Kip!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's just that we bought a hibachi together, and then he ran off and got married, and things got pretty ugly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, let me ask you something, was Kip a better roommate than me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Aw, don't do that
Phoebe's Massage Parlor, Phoebe's assistant is telling her about the changes to her schedule.

lines.speaker: Phoebe's Assistant
lines.text: We've got a couple changes in your schedule. Your 4:00 herbal massage has been pushed back to 4:30 and Miss Somerfield canceled her 5:30 shiatsu.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, thanks. (assistant leaves, then walks back in)

lines.speaker: Phoebe's Assistant
lines.text: Oh, here comes your 3:00. I don't mean to sound unprofessional, but, yum (walks out, Paolo enters)

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Buon Giorno, Bella Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, Paolo, hi, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Uh, Racquela tell me you massage, eh?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, Racquela's right, yeah!
Paolo speaks Italian

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, I don't know what you just said, so let's get started.

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Uh, I am, uh, being naked?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, that's really your decision, I mean, some people prefer, you know, to take off...oh whoops! You're being naked!
Central Perk, everyone but Phoebe is there.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (to Ross) I can't believe you don't want to know. I mean, I couldn't not know, I mean, if, if the doctor knows, and Carol knows, and Susan knows....

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: And Monica knows...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha, heh, how could you know, I don't even know!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Carol called me to thank me for the lasagna, I asked, she told me.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what's it gonna be? (Monica whispers in Joey's ear. Ross gets up and waves arms frantically in protest)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wait-oh-hey-huh, oh great now he knows, and I don't know!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm sorry, I'm just excited about being an aunt!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Or an uncle...
Phoebe enters

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Hey Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Fine!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Nothing, I'm sorry, I'm just, I'm out of sorts.

lines.speaker: Customer
lines.text: Hey, can we get some cappuccino over here?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, right, that's me!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Chandler, that table place closes at 7, come on.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Fine. (Joey and Chandler walk towards the door)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Phoebe, what is it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, you know Paolo?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm familiar with his work, yes...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he made a move on me.
Joey and Chandler come back

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa, store will be open tomorrow!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: More coffee over here, please!
Commercial Break
Central Perk, continued from earlier.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well, what happened?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, he came in for a massage, and everything was fine until. (A flashback starts Paolo, lying on massage table, moving his hands up Phoebe's legs.)
[Cut back to Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Ooooohh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My God.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you sure?
The flashback resumes with Paolo grabbing her butt.
[Cut back to Central Perk.]

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh yeah, I'm sure. (Flashback resumes with Phoebe doing a voiceover.) And all of a sudden his hands weren't the problem anymore. (Flashback continues: Paolo rolls over, Phoebe looks down, then quickly looks up, bites lip, shakes her head)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Was it...?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, boy scouts could have camped under there.

lines.speaker: Guys
lines.text: Oooooo....
Rachel runs over

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: "Ooo," what?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Uma Thurman.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The actress!
all talking indistinctly, high-fiving

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Thanks Rach.
Rachel walks away

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what are you gonna do?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You have to tell her! You have to tell her! It's your moral obligation, as a friend, as a woman, I think it's a feminist issue! Guys? Guys? (waiting for guys to chime in)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, yeah, you have to tell her.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Feminist issue. That's where I went!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: She is gonna hate me.
Ross:(sympathetic yet...) Yeah, well...
The Table Store, Joey and Chandler and looking for their new table.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Will you pick one, just pick one! Here, how about that one? (points to a table)

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's patio furniture!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So what, like people are gonna come in and think, "Uh-oh, I'm outside again?" Of course!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (gesturing towards another table) What about the birds?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know, birds just don't say, "Hello, sit here, eat something."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You pick one.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right, how about the ladybugs?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, so, forget about the birds, but big red insects suggest fine dining!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Fine, you want to get the birds, get the birds!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Not like that, I won't! (pauses) Kip would have liked the birds! (Joey turns and gives Chandler a dirty look)
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel folding and packing clothes in suitcases as Phoebe enters.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you moving out?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, these aren't all my suitcases. (picks up small blue suitcase and shows to Phoebe) This one's Paolo's.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, um, Rachel can we talk for a sec?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, sure...just a sec, though, 'cause Paolo's on his way over.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! (sits down) Ok, um, ok, um,

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Pheebs, Pheebs...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, um, (clears throat) we haven't known each other for that long a time, and, um, there are three things that you should know about me. One, my friends are the most important thing in my life, two, I never lie, and three, I make the best oatmeal raisin cookies in the world. (Phoebe opens a tin and offers Rachel a cookie)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (taking cookie) Ok, thanks Pheebs (takes bite of cookie, overwhelmed) Oh my God, why have I never tasted these before?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, I don't make them a lot because I don't think it's fair to the other cookies

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All right, well, you're right, these are the best oatmeal cookies I've ever had.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Which proves that I never lie.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I guess you don't.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Paolo made a pass at me.
Rachel looks stunned
Chandler and Joey's, Ross, Chandler, Joey, and Monica admiring their new table.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So, what do you think?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I think It's the most beautiful table I've ever seen.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I know!
The camera pans back to reveal Joey and Chandler's new foosball table.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: So how does this work, you going to balance the plates on these little guys' heads?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Who cares, we'll eat at the sink! Come on, let's play!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Heads up Ross! (Monica scores on Chandler and Joey) Score! (points at Chandler) You suck!
Chandler looks at Joey in amazement
Monica and Rachel's, Rachel is recovering from the shock.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I need some milk.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ok, I've got milk (takes thermos from her bag and starts to pour a cup) Here you go... (Rachel drinks straight from thermos) Oh!(Rachel finishes thermos) Better?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No...oh, I feel so stupid! Oh, I think about the other day with you guys and I was all "Oh, Paolo, he's so great, he makes me feel so..." Oh, God, I'm so embarrassed!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so embarrassed, I'm the one he hit on!
Phoebe's and Rachel's lines overlap

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Pheebs, if I had never met him this never would have happened!

lines.speaker: Rachel and Phoebe
lines.text: I'm so sorry! No I'm sorry! No I'm sorry! No I'm sorry!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: No, wait, oh, what are we sorry about?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't know...right, he's the pig!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Such a pig!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God, he's such a pig,

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh he's like a...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: He's like a big disgusting...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: ...like a...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...pig...pig man!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, good! Ok...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (voice wavers) Oh, but he was my pig man...how did I not see this?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (raises hand) Oh! I know! (Rachel startled) It's because... he's gorgeous, and he's charming, and when he looks at you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ok, Ok, Pheebs...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The end.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, God...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Should I not have told you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, no, trust, me, it's, it's, it's much better that I know. Uh, I just liked it better before it was better...
Phoebe scoots her chair over to Rachel and hugs her
Chandler and Joey's, Phoebe is telling everyone how it went across the hall as the foosball game continues.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I think she took it pretty well. You know Paolo's over there right now, so...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We should get over there and see if she's okay. (switching places with Ross) Just one...second! Score! (Monica scores, high-fives with Ross) Game! Come on. (Monica and Phoebe leave)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (wiping his brow) Ah...ooh! Well, looks like, uh, we kicked your butts.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No-no, she kicked our butts. You could be on the Olympic standing-there team.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come on, two on one.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What are you still doing here? She just broke up with the guy, it's time for you to swoop in!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What, now?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yes, now is when you swoop! You gotta make sure that when Paolo walks out of there, the first guy Rachel sees is you, She's gotta know that you're everything he's not! You're like, like the anti-Paolo!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: My Catholic friend is right. She's distraught. You're there for her. You pick up the pieces, and then you usher in the age of Ross! (Ross and Chandler look off into the distance. Joey, wondering what they are looking at, looks in the same direction)
Monica and Rachel's Balcony, Rachel is throwing Paolo's clothes over the side.

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: No, that's cold, that's cold, that's...
[Cut to inside the apartment.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (entering) How's it going?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Don't stare. Now she just finished throwing his clothes off the balcony, now there's just a lot of gesturing and arm-waving, (shows Rachel gesturing with hands in front of her chest), Ok, that is either, "How could you?" or, "Enormous breasts!" Here he comes!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh!
Paolo enters. Ross, Phoebe, and Monica scatter

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Uh, I am, uh, to say good-bye.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, ok bye-bye.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Paolo, I really hate you for what you did to Rachel, (hands him a lasagna) but I still have five of these, so heat it at 375 until the cheese bubbles.

lines.speaker: Paolo
lines.text: Grazie.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Paolo, I-I just want to tell you and I think I speak for everyone when I say... (shuts door in his face and walks away)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, just look at her... (girls move toward Rachel on the balcony)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh you guys, I-I really think just one of us should go out there so she's not overwhelmed...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, you're right.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pulls Monica back) ...and I really think it should be me.
[Cut to the balcony, Ross has just climbed through the window.]

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You all right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, I've been better...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Come here. (he hugs Rachel) Listen, you deserve so much better than him...you know, I mean, you, you, you should be with a guy who knows what he has when he has you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, Ross...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am so sick of guys. I don't want to look at another guy, I don't want to think about another guy, I don't even want to be near another guy. (Ross crosses arms)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Huh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh Ross, you're so great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ohhhh (Hugs her and sighs)
[Cut to inside the apartment, Rachel and Ross are entering.]

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ooh...hey honey, are you all right?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You ok?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: ...medium...hmm...any cookies left?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yep!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: See, Rach, uh, see, I don't think that swearing off guys altogether is the answer. I really don't. I think that what you need is to develop a more sophisticated screening process.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No. I just need to be by myself for a while, you know? I just got to figure out what I want

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh, no, no, see, because not...not all guys are going to be a Paolo.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, I know, I know, and I'm sure your little boy is not going to grow up to be one.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (astonished) What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I-I'm, I'm having a boy?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh...no. No, no, in fact, you're not having a boy.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Wha-I'm having, I'm having a boy! (babbling) Huh, am I having a boy?

lines.speaker: Girls
lines.text: Yes, you're having a boy! (Monica runs over and hugs Ross)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm having a boy! Oh, I'm having a boy!
Joey and Chandler run in

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wha-

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wha-

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm having a boy! I-I'm having a boy!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: We already knew that! (they hug)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm having a son. Um...
Ross looks scared
Closing Credits
Chandler and Joey's, Monica is busy killing Chandle and Joey at foosball.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Yes! And that would be a shut-down!

lines.speaker: Joey and Chandler
lines.text: Shut-out!! (They both start heading for their rooms.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Where are you guys going? Come on, one more game!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Uh, it's 2:30 in the morning!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, get out!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You guys are always hanging out in my apartment! Come on, I'll only use my left hand, huh? Come on, wussies! (Joey and Chandler pick her up) All right, ok, I gotta go. I'm going, (they throw her out) and I'm gone.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (to Joey) One more game?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh yeah!
End
